pattern: A write pointer advances only when the current value differs from the last written value, compacting unique elements to the front of a sorted array in a single pass.
hints: Because the array is sorted, all duplicates of a value are adjacent, so you only need to compare each element to the most recently accepted one.
Track a slot index for the next unique position; when a new distinct value appears, write it there and advance the slot.
After the pass, truncate the array to the slot count so the mutated argument reflects only the unique prefix.

edge_inputs.description: all elements identical collapses to one

stress.description: large sorted array over a narrow value range ensuring many duplicates
stress.note: narrow range over large n guarantees many duplicate runs
